The plan preserves the known Codex blocker: Firestore Emulator JAR download failed with 403 Forbidden and tests later hit ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:8080. Current PR does not change Runtime, functions, firestore.rules, firebase.json, package files, .github workflows, public assets or Unity files.

entries.goal: Check the Beta 1 Emulator Tests workflow/run status after the TODO_INDEX fix and document whether real emulator results are available.
entries.notes: No fake green result: .github/workflows/beta1-emulator-tests.yml is not present in this checkout, no git remote is configured, gh is unavailable, and direct GitHub shell access is blocked by CONNECT tunnel 403. No real Beta 1 Emulator Tests run, run ID, logs, Firestore Emulator startup, beta1:rules execution, or beta1:callable execution could be verified here. Known critical blocker remains Firestore Emulator JAR download 403 Forbidden followed by ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:8080 until a real workflow_dispatch or PR run proves otherwise. Runtime files, functions, Firestore rules, firebase config, workflows, package files, public assets, Unity, secrets, deploys, and production Firebase resources remained untouched.

entries.goal: Fix only the failing PR #178 Beta-1 Firestore Rules emulator test without loosening rules or changing runtime product logic.
entries.notes: Identified firestore.rules line 345 as the glitchSafetyRules deny-all rule. The beta1 Firestore rules test incorrectly included glitchSafetyRules in the generic owner-readable loop, while later also asserting that the same document must be unreadable. The test now keeps glitchSafetyRules out of the owner-readable loop and explicitly asserts denied read/create/update/delete. GitHub Actions reported Firestore/Functions emulator startup succeeded; the old CI JAR 403 blocker is not the latest CI cause. Local emulator execution is still blocked in this container by JAR download 403, so PR #178 GitHub Actions must rerun.

entries.goal: Document the successful Beta-1 GitHub Actions emulator verification after PR #178 merge in runbooks and project registers.
entries.notes: Documentation/register-only update. PR #178 is merged (merge SHA f3643800272dd152b3d6a6d3811a6229522e7cc3; successful pre-merge head SHA e79f21ffbd5558aa8efa5936093faf63d522b7a4). GitHub Actions reported Build success, Beta 1 Emulator Tests success, job 'Beta-1 Firestore and callable emulator tests' success, and step 'Run focused Beta-1 emulator suites' success. Firestore and Functions emulators started in CI. No runtime files were changed, no secrets used, no deploy triggered, and no production Firebase resources were used.

entries.goal: Harden Beta-1 admin panel client-side validation, per-form UX/error handling, and claim-guard states without backend authority changes.
entries.notes: Added payload prevalidation for mission create/publish, checkpoint create, glitch schedule/cancel, safety review, and XP adjust in admin client UI flow; each form now has independent loading/success/error feedback and sanitized error text only. No new Firebase Functions, no Firestore Rules changes, no .github changes, and no client final authority introduced; existing server callables remain final authority.

entries.goal: Document a controlled execution plan for Agent-Admin Phase 1, server role/audit closure, pilot readiness sprint, AT closed beta 25-50, and staged runtime-autonomy expansion.
entries.notes: Planning/docs/register-only update. Added a single leading plan with ordered phases, acceptance criteria, inclusion/exclusion criteria, and stop conditions. No runtime product code, functions, firestore rules, secrets, deploys, Unity, or protected-scope authority changes.

entries.goal: Fix post-merge Beta-1 smoke template/client-preview validation mismatches from PR #186 follow-up.
entries.notes: Updated smoke mission type to movement and glitch window to 10 minutes to match client validation constraints. Hardened read guard so non-numeric/missing mission rewardXp stays undefined and UI now renders 'Reward offen' unless rewardXp is numeric (including valid 0). No Functions/Rules/.github changes and no client-final-authority introduction.

Firestore rules keep client writes blocked. Audit write is mandatory per action path; runtime autonomy remains NOT fully activated (no auto-run, no auto-merge, no auto-deploy).
